core.when: When implementing SGDRegressor for federated LGD estimation
core.action: Set random_state parameter explicitly to verify reproducibility across federated nodes

consequence.description: Without random_state, each lgdModel call produces non-deterministic results due to random data shuffling and weight initialization. This breaks federated learning convergence guarantees as different nodes will reach different local minima.

evidence_refs.summary: lgdModel.py:60-61 initializes SGDRegressor without random_state parameter; SGDRegressor defaults to shuffling=True and random_weight_init=True when random_state is None

core.when: When implementing the cold/warm start toggle logic
core.action: Verify both intercept and coef parameters are provided together for warm-start mode

consequence.description: The condition 'if intercept is None or coef is None' triggers cold-start if either parameter is missing. Partial initialization with only one parameter will silently fall back to random initialization, producing incorrect model updates in the federated loop.

evidence_refs.summary: lgdModel.py:78 uses OR condition for None checking: 'if intercept is None or coef is None'

validation_threshold: grep -c "or coef is None" lgdModel.py >= 1 → PASS

core.when: When providing input data to lgdModel
core.action: Verify data source contains columns exactly named 'X' (explanatory) and 'Y' (target)

consequence.description: lgdModel accesses df[['X']] and df['Y'] without validation. Missing or misnamed columns will raise KeyError at runtime, breaking both standalone and federated execution flows.

evidence_refs.summary: lgdModel.py:73-75 directly access df[['X']] and df['Y'] with no column validation; dataSource.py:29 expects regression_data.csv with these exact column names
lgdModel.py:73-75 directly access df[['X']] and df['Y'] with no column validation; dataSource.py:29 expects regression_data.csv with these exact column names

core.when: When implementing federated parameter averaging logic
core.action: Iterate over each participating servers (k from 1 to n) when computing weighted average

consequence.description: federated_run.py uses weights[str(n)] for all servers instead of weights[str(k)] for each server k. This causes the averaged parameters to use only the last server's weight, corrupting federated model convergence and producing incorrect global LGD estimates.

evidence_refs.summary: federated_run.py:64-66 incorrectly uses weights[str(n)] instead of weights[str(k)] in averaging loop; same bug at lines 89-91

validation_threshold: grep "weights\[str(n)\]\|weights\[str(k)\]" federated_run.py | wc -l >= 4 → FAIL if pattern 'str(k)' absent in avg loop

core.action: Use ports other than 5001-5004 for the default configuration without updating both server and client code

consequence.description: model_server.py:35 derives server ID as 'int(port) - 5000', and Federated_Demo.md documents ports 5001-5004. Port mismatches between server and controller cause requests to reach wrong servers, breaking federated coordination.

core.when: When selecting regression algorithm for LGD estimation
core.action: Accept that SGDRegressor is the only available algorithm (not abstracted or configurable)

consequence.description: The regression algorithm is hardcoded to sklearn.linear_model.SGDRegressor. Replacing it requires modifying lgdModel.py directly. This creates a tight coupling and prevents using alternative algorithms (e.g., ridge regression, ElasticNet) without code changes.

evidence_refs.summary: lgdModel.py:60 'clf = linear_model.SGDRegressor(...)' with no abstraction layer; blueprint explicitly documents this as a replaceable point boundary

core.when: When running lgdModel in iterative fashion for federated learning
core.action: Set max_iter=1 to verify exactly one gradient step per function call

consequence.description: The max_iter=1 setting is critical for the federated learning architecture where each call represents one epoch. Increasing max_iter would perform multiple gradient steps per call, breaking the per-epoch parameter update contract required by federated averaging.

evidence_refs.summary: lgdModel.py:60 sets max_iter=1 explicitly; blueprint acceptance hint confirms 'max_iter=1 setting produces exactly one gradient step per call'

core.when: When returning fitted parameters from lgdModel
core.action: Return dict with keys 'intercept' and 'coefficient' containing scalar values

consequence.description: model_server.py and standalone_run.py access params['intercept'] and params['coefficient']. Returning different key names (e.g., 'coef' or 'coefficients') would cause KeyError in all downstream consumers, breaking both standalone and federated modes.

evidence_refs.summary: lgdModel.py:84 returns {'intercept': ..., 'coefficient': ...}; model_server.py:44-45 and standalone_run.py:30-31 access these exact keys
lgdModel.py:84 returns {'intercept': ..., 'coefficient': ...}; model_server.py:44-45 and standalone_run.py:30-31 access these exact keys
lgdModel.py:84 returns {'intercept': ..., 'coefficient': ...}; model_server.py:44-45 and standalone_run.py:30-31 access these exact keys

core.when: When claiming LGD estimation capabilities
core.action: Claim statistical rigor equivalent to pooled dataset analysis

consequence.description: Federated LGD estimation with SGD produces parameters that may not converge to the pooled optimum due to data heterogeneity across servers. Presenting federated estimates as equivalent to centralized estimation would misrepresent the statistical properties of the model.

evidence_refs.summary: README.md:18 states 'early alpha release' indicating experimental status; federated architecture introduces non-convergence risk from data heterogeneity not present in centralized estimation

core.when: When considering replacing the SGDRegressor implementation
core.action: Claim that federated learning produces identical results to centralized estimation

consequence.description: Federated averaging with SGD is an approximation that depends on data distribution across servers. Different server configurations will produce different model parameters even with identical hyperparameters, which is expected behavior, not a bug.

evidence_refs.summary: SGD nature implies convergence to local optimum; Federated_Demo.md:77 describes iterative estimation with averaged parameters sent back to servers

core.when: When evaluating federated averaging convergence
core.action: Skip monitoring parameter stability across epochs

consequence.description: Without tracking parameter change magnitude across epochs, users cannot determine if the federated process has converged. Parameters oscillating or diverging indicate misconfiguration or data quality issues that would go unnoticed.

evidence_refs.summary: federated_run.py:76 prints epoch number but does not compute parameter delta or convergence metric; SGDRegressor tol=None disables sklearn's built-in convergence check

core.when: When performing initial cold-start call
core.action: Pass None for both intercept and coef parameters

consequence.description: The first federated iteration requires random initialization. Passing non-None values on cold-start would improperly seed the federated process with arbitrary values, corrupting the initial global model state.

evidence_refs.summary: lgdModel.py:78 cold-start condition requires intercept is None or coef is None; standalone_run.py:26 demonstrates correct cold-start call with no init parameters
lgdModel.py:78 cold-start condition requires intercept is None or coef is None; standalone_run.py:26 demonstrates correct cold-start call with no init parameters

core.when: When implementing the initial parameter averaging loop
core.action: skip the first server by starting loop index at 1 instead of 0

consequence.description: The first server's parameters are excluded from initial averaging, causing all subsequent averaged parameters to be incorrect and breaking federated convergence. Server 0 contribution is completely lost.

evidence_refs.summary: federated_run.py:64 - 'for k in range(1, n):' skips k=0, excluding server 1's parameters from initial averaging. The weights dict expects keys '1', '2', etc. but loop starts at 1, missing server '0' w

validation_threshold: grep -n 'for k in range(1, n)' federated_run.py && echo 'BUG: Loop starts at k=1, missing first server' || echo 'OK'

core.when: When implementing the epoch averaging loop
core.action: use the loop variable k to index weights instead of using the final index n

consequence.description: Epoch averaging uses weights[str(n)] inside the loop instead of weights[str(k)], causing only the last server's weight (0.0 for n=4) to be applied repeatedly, producing meaningless averaged parameters.

evidence_refs.summary: federated_run.py:89-91 - 'for k in range(n): avg_coef += weights[str(n)] * coeffs[str(n)]' incorrectly uses str(n) instead of str(k) for both weights and coeffs. Since weights has no key '4' (only '1'

validation_threshold: grep -n 'weights\[str(n)\]' federated_run.py && echo 'BUG: Should use str(k)' || echo 'OK'

core.when: When initializing SGDRegressor with warm start parameters
core.action: pass intercept_init and coef_init to the fit() method of sklearn SGDRegressor

consequence.description: SGDRegressor.fit() does not accept intercept_init or coef_init parameters. Passing these will raise a TypeError, breaking all federated update cycles and preventing convergence.

evidence_refs.summary: lgdModel.py:81 - 'clf.fit(X, y, intercept_init=intercept, coef_init=coef)' is invalid. SGDRegressor uses set_params() for initialization, not fit() parameters. See sklearn documentation for SGDRegress

validation_threshold: grep -n 'intercept_init\|coef_init' lgdModel.py && echo 'BUG: Invalid SGDRegressor parameters' || echo 'OK'

core.when: When making HTTP requests to federated servers
core.action: include timeout parameters to prevent indefinite blocking on unreachable servers

consequence.description: Without HTTP timeouts, a single unresponsive server causes the entire federated run to hang indefinitely. In production, this blocks all participating servers waiting for the coordinator.

evidence_refs.summary: federated_run.py:47,55,80 - All requests.get() and requests.post() calls lack timeout parameters. requests library defaults to indefinite blocking.

core.when: When configuring server weights for federated averaging
core.action: dynamically calculate weights based on actual data volumes or sample counts per server

consequence.description: Equal weighting assumes equal data volumes across servers. If servers have unequal data (e.g., 100 vs 10000 samples), the weighted average under-represents larger datasets, producing biased LGD estimates that misrepresent actual credit risk.

evidence_refs.summary: federated_run.py:33-34 - TODO comment acknowledges hardcoded weights are incorrect. Line 34: weights = {'1': 0.25, '2': 0.25, '3': 0.25, '4': 0.25} assumes equal data volumes.

validation_threshold: grep -n 'weights = {' federated_run.py | grep -v '# TODO' && echo 'HARDCODED_WEIGHTS' || echo 'DYNAMIC'

core.when: When running Flask model servers in production
core.action: run with debug=True enabled in production environments

consequence.description: Flask debug mode enables code reloading and Werkzeug debugger, exposing the Python traceback to attackers. This creates remote code execution vulnerabilities in production deployments.

evidence_refs.summary: model_server.py:81 - 'app.run(debug=True)' enables Werkzeug debugger. This is acceptable for demo but the pattern is present and could be used in deployment.

core.when: When configuring the number of servers in config.yml
core.action: verify the server count matches exactly the number of weights defined in the weights dictionary

consequence.description: The weights dictionary is hardcoded for 4 servers. If config.yml specifies servers != 4, the URL construction and weight indexing will fail, causing KeyError or IndexError exceptions.

evidence_refs.summary: federated_run.py:34 - weights dict has exactly 4 entries. Line 40-42 constructs URL list based on config['servers']. Line 30: n = config['servers'] but weights are hardcoded.

core.when: When presenting federated learning results

core.when: When processing JSON responses from federated servers
core.action: validate response structure before accessing dictionary keys

consequence.description: Without validation, if a server returns malformed JSON or missing keys ('coefficient', 'intercept'), the code raises KeyError, crashing the entire federated run mid-epoch.

evidence_refs.summary: federated_run.py:58,59,82,83 - Direct dictionary access without validation: data['coefficient'], data['intercept']. No try/except around JSON parsing.

core.when: When handling HTTP errors from server communication
core.action: check HTTP status codes and implement retry logic for transient failures

consequence.description: Network partitions, server overload, or temporary unavailability cause HTTP errors that crash the federated run. Without error handling, a single epoch failure prevents any parameter updates from being applied.

evidence_refs.summary: federated_run.py:47,55,80 - No status code checking (if res.status_code == 200) and no try/except around requests calls.

core.when: When scaling the federated system to more than 4 servers
core.action: assume the hardcoded weights dictionary remains valid without modification

consequence.description: Increasing servers > 4 in config.yml causes KeyError when accessing weights beyond the 4 hardcoded keys, crashing the federated run.

evidence_refs.summary: federated_run.py:34 - weights = {'1': 0.25, '2': 0.25, '3': 0.25, '4': 0.25}. No programmatic generation of weights based on server count.

core.when: When implementing SGDRegressor warm-start parameter initialization
core.action: Use sklearn set_params() method to set initial coefficient and intercept values before fitting with warm_start=True

consequence.description: SGDRegressor.fit() does not accept intercept_init and coef_init keyword arguments, causing TypeError at runtime when warm-starting with pre-existing parameter values

evidence_refs.summary: lgdModel.py:81 uses clf.fit(X, y, intercept_init=intercept, coef_init=coef) but sklearn SGDRegressor.fit() does not support these parameters

validation_threshold: grep 'intercept_init\|coef_init' lgdModel.py → FAIL (API mismatch with sklearn)

core.when: When implementing warm-start SGDRegressor with external parameter initialization
core.action: Set clf.coef_ and clf.intercept_ attributes directly before calling fit(), or use partial_fit() for stateful updates

consequence.description: Attempting to pass custom initial parameters via non-existent fit() arguments will raise TypeError, breaking the epoch iteration loop

evidence_refs.summary: lgdModel.py:81 invokes non-existent sklearn parameters, documented in sklearn SGDRegressor API which only supports sample_weight via fit() kwargs

core.when: When configuring SGDRegressor for iterative model estimation
core.action: Set warm_start=True to enable parameter reuse across consecutive fit() calls

consequence.description: With warm_start=False, each fit() call resets coefficients to random initialization, preventing convergence across epochs and producing non-monotonic parameter estimates

evidence_refs.summary: lgdModel.py:60 sets warm_start=False while lgdModel.py:81 attempts to pass initial parameters, contradicting the documented warm_start behavior for SGDRegressor
lgdModel.py:60 sets warm_start=False while lgdModel.py:81 attempts to pass initial parameters, contradicting the documented warm_start behavior for SGDRegressor

core.when: When preparing CSV data for LGD model estimation
core.action: Verify data files contain exactly two columns named 'X' (target variable) and 'Y' (explanatory variable) without missing values

consequence.description: Mismatched column names or missing values will cause KeyError during DataFrame extraction or produce NaN coefficients, invalidating the LGD estimation

core.when: When running standalone execution for environment validation
core.action: Execute standalone_run.py first to verify paths, dependencies, and core estimation logic before launching federated servers

core.action: Hardcode Epochs value in standalone_run.py when it should be configurable via config.yml like federated_run.py

core.when: When executing a federated LGD training epoch
core.action: Call /start (cold-start) before any /update (warm-start) calls, as the model requires initial parameters to be established first

core.when: When implementing the SGDRegressor-based LGD model iteration
core.action: Set max_iter=1 and tol=None to enforce single-epoch per fit() call, as each gradient step must be performed independently across federated nodes

consequence.description: Multi-epoch convergence within a single fit() call breaks the federated averaging contract, causing incorrect parameter aggregation across nodes

evidence_refs.summary: lgdModel.py:60 (SGDRegressor with max_iter=1, tol=None) enforces single-step iteration design

core.when: When completing a federated averaging round before sending parameters to the next epoch
core.action: Complete the averaging of each node parameters before distributing averaged params to any node, as premature sending of partial averages corrupts model convergence

consequence.description: Partial averages sent to nodes cause parameter drift and non-convergence in subsequent epochs, producing invalid LGD model coefficients

evidence_refs.summary: federated_run.py:86-92 shows averaging loop completing before data is reassigned for next epoch at line 92

core.when: When configuring federated averaging weights across model servers
core.action: Adjust server weights proportionally when changing the number of participating servers, as the default 0.25 weight assumes exactly 4 equal-data-volume nodes

core.when: When implementing or refactoring model initialization logic in lgdModel.py
core.action: Assume parameters can be passed as None when intending to use existing values — always distinguish between 'parameter not provided' (use existing) and 'parameter explicitly set to None' (cold start)

consequence.description: Confusing None vs not-present causes silent cold starts that reset model state, producing incorrect LGD estimates and invalidating credit risk calculations

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-006

core.when: When implementing federated protocol communication between coordinator and servers
core.action: Exchange only the two scalar parameters (intercept and coefficient) per communication round — must NOT add gradient vectors, Hessian information, or additional statistics to the payload

consequence.description: Adding extra parameters to federated exchanges increases bandwidth requirements and attack surface for parameter tampering, violating the minimal payload design essential for bandwidth-constrained environments

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-028

core.when: When implementing data acquisition for LGD model training
core.action: Query the openNPL API endpoint /api/npl_data/counterparties for structured entity data including financial metrics — must NOT use alternative data sources without validation against openNPL schema

consequence.description: Using mismatched data sources causes schema incompatibilities with downstream LGD estimation, potentially producing meaningless regression results or silent data corruption

evidence_refs.summary: Derived from BD: BD-043

core.when: When modifying training epoch configuration across the federated learning system
core.action: Update epoch count in both config.yml and standalone_run.py simultaneously — implement a centralized constant or import from a shared module to prevent dual maintenance drift

consequence.description: Updating epochs in only one location causes divergent training duration between federated and standalone modes, invalidating comparative results and producing non-reproducible experiments

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-081

core.when: When implementing or evaluating federated learning architecture decisions
core.action: Verify raw data remains local to each server node and only model parameters (intercept and coefficient) traverse the network — must NOT implement centralized data pooling even if technically feasible

consequence.description: Centralizing raw financial data violates data sovereignty requirements for multi-institution scenarios, causing regulatory non-compliance with GDPR, banking secrecy laws, and institutional data sharing prohibitions

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-019

core.when: When implementing FedAvg aggregation logic in federated_run.py
core.action: Fetch actual node data shapes (sample counts) via controlled API and apply weighted averaging proportional to local data volumes — must NOT use hardcoded equal weights (0.25 each) in production environments

consequence.description: Using equal weights when datasets have heterogeneous sizes causes model convergence bias toward smaller nodes, producing suboptimal LGD estimates that systematically underestimate risk for larger institutions

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-059

core.when: When implementing federated coordination and model aggregation in federated_run.py
core.action: Use Federated Averaging (FedAvg) algorithm with synchronous rounds and parameter-level averaging only — must NOT implement asynchronous averaging, differential privacy mechanisms, or secure aggregation without explicit architectural approval

consequence.description: Using alternative aggregation methods without architectural review breaks the synchronous FedAvg assumption, potentially causing parameter staleness, convergence failures, or compatibility issues with existing server implementations

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-012

core.when: When implementing data loading and regression preparation in lgdModel.py and dataSource.py
core.action: Extract column 'X' as target values and column 'Y' as explanatory variables in the exact order specified at lgdModel.py:73-75 — must NOT swap, rename, or use alternative column mappings

consequence.description: Inverting the X/Y column convention produces an inverted regression model where target and predictor variables are swapped, causing completely incorrect LGD estimates and invalid credit risk assessments

evidence_refs.summary: Derived from BD: BD-078

core.when: When importing and using the lgdModel module for standalone execution
core.action: Import lgdModel directly via 'from lgdModel import lgdModel' to verify standalone execution without network dependencies; do not introduce HTTP client abstractions that would couple core estimation to the model_server layer

consequence.description: Refactoring to use HTTP client for lgdModel would break standalone execution, preventing unit testing and local development without network infrastructure

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-018

core.when: When implementing linear regression for LGD estimation in federated learning
core.action: Use SGDRegressor from scikit-learn with partial_fit() for incremental learning; do not replace with OLS closed-form solution or other batch-only algorithms that require centralized data

consequence.description: Switching to OLS or batch-only regression breaks the federated learning architecture, requiring centralized data aggregation that violates distributed processing assumptions

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-020

core.when: When implementing LGD estimation logic
core.action: Use SGDRegressor as the regression algorithm; be aware that switching to alternative algorithms (e.g., RandomForest, neural networks) requires implementing abstract base class, factory pattern, and serialization abstraction layers

consequence.description: Hardcoded SGDRegressor assumption means alternative algorithms require significant refactoring; strategy accuracy depends on regression model choice and must be validated independently

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-007

core.when: When parsing YAML configuration files in federated_run.py
core.action: Use ruamel.yaml with safe loading (typ='safe') for YAML parsing; never use yaml.load without specifying a safe Loader to prevent arbitrary code execution through YAML deserialization vulnerabilities

consequence.description: Unsafe YAML loading allows arbitrary code execution from malicious configuration files, creating remote code execution vulnerability in production deployments

evidence_refs.summary: Derived from BD: BD-058

core.when: When configuring federated learning server infrastructure
core.action: Use explicit server ID configuration via environment variable instead of port-derived ID (n = port - 5000); verify port availability in the 5001-5004 range before startup; implement graceful degradation when ports are unavailable

consequence.description: Port-derived server ID creates cascading failure risk where port conflicts prevent server startup, causing health check failures that halt entire federated execution

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-084

core.when: When implementing federated learning parameter synchronization across distributed servers
core.action: Implement per-epoch parameter collection and averaging with explicit epochs configuration; verify each server's partial_fit() results are collected and averaged before the next round begins

consequence.description: Missing per-epoch synchronization causes parameter drift across servers, leading to inconsistent model states and failed federated convergence

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-014

validation_threshold: epochs == None → FAIL; epochs >= 1 AND epochs <= 100 → WARN; epochs explicitly configured → PASS

core.when: When implementing model training with partial_fit() in lgdModel.py
core.action: Use max_iter=1 for each partial_fit() call to maintain explicit per-epoch control in the federated orchestration loop — do not change to higher values as this blurs the distinction between local and global epochs

consequence.description: Increasing max_iter beyond 1 causes local optimization iterations to blend with global federated rounds, making convergence analysis unreliable and breaking the federated coordination contract

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-021

core.when: When implementing server ID derivation in model_server.py
core.action: Assume consecutive port allocation starting from 5001 for server ID derivation — use explicit server ID configuration instead

consequence.description: Port-based ID derivation assumes a specific port numbering scheme that may not hold in all deployment scenarios, causing server ID mismatches when ports are allocated non-consecutively

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-008

core.when: When designing APIs for model serving endpoints
core.action: Implement external state management for distributed deployments — the /, /start, /update three-endpoint design assumes stateless operation and does not handle distributed state coordination

consequence.description: Stateless API design fails in distributed scenarios where multiple server instances require coordination, causing inconsistent state across requests

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-011

core.when: When implementing server lifecycle management
core.action: Implement the /stop endpoint for graceful shutdown to verify in-flight requests complete and state is properly finalized before termination

consequence.description: Abrupt server termination without graceful shutdown risks data corruption and leaves clients with incomplete responses

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-047

core.when: When setting up project dependencies
core.action: Use virtual environment isolation for dependency management to prevent conflicts with system packages and verify reproducible builds

consequence.description: System-wide package installation risks breaking system packages and causes dependency conflicts across projects

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-049

core.when: When integrating with sklearn utilities or ML pipelines
core.action: Use standard sklearn convention where X=features and y=target — the code uses reversed convention with X as target and y as explanatory variables

consequence.description: Reversed X/y convention causes silent failures when using sklearn utilities expecting standard ordering, producing incorrect model predictions or cryptic errors

evidence_refs.summary: Derived from BD: BD-068

core.when: When implementing federated round orchestration with sequential blocking
core.action: Implement timeout handling and retry logic for individual server calls — sequential blocking with BD-072 (/start before /update) and BD-074 (averaging before next epoch) creates cascading deadlock if any server becomes unresponsive mid-round

consequence.description: A single slow or unresponsive server during /start or /update blocks the entire federated round with no timeout mechanism, causing cascading timeouts across all rounds

evidence_refs.summary: Derived from BD: BD-087

core.when: When configuring training epochs for LGD model
core.action: Centralize epoch configuration in config.yml and import from it in both standalone_run.py and federated_run.py — do not hardcode epoch values separately

consequence.description: Dual-hardcoded epoch values create maintenance hazard; updating epochs in one file but not the other causes federated and standalone modes to train for different durations, invalidating BD-055 validation baseline

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-088

core.when: When parsing server port numbers to derive server IDs
core.action: Hardcode the magic formula n = int(port) - 5000 — the port-to-ID mapping depends on a specific port range allocation that must remain consistent

consequence.description: Hardcoded port offset makes server ID derivation brittle; changing port allocation scheme breaks ID mapping silently throughout the system

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-075

core.when: When refactoring training loop code
core.action: Consider extracting the shared SGD training loop from standalone_run.py and federated_run.py into a common module to eliminate duplication — duplicate logic in epoch_loop across both files creates maintenance risk

consequence.description: Identical training loop logic duplicated in two files requires synchronized updates; changes applied to one file but not the other cause divergent behavior between modes

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-079

core.when: When implementing federated learning fit logic using partial_fit() calls
core.action: Enable warm_start on the SGDRegressor — warm_start must remain False to verify each partial_fit() call starts fresh without leveraging optimizer state from previous iterations

consequence.description: Setting warm_start=True preserves optimizer state across partial_fit() calls, causing unintended state carryover between federated rounds and breaking the parameter averaging protocol semantics

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-052

core.when: When selecting features for Loss Given Default (LGD) credit risk estimation
core.action: Verify that current_assets and cash_and_cash_equivalent_items are the intended features — if replacing with alternative features, verify liquidity characteristics are still captured as these are fundamental to credit risk modeling

consequence.description: Using alternative features without liquidity coverage may cause the LGD model to underestimate default losses for asset-heavy borrowers, leading to insufficient provision calculations in live trading

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-044

core.when: When implementing or refactoring federated learning aggregation logic
core.action: Verify federated averaging operation (parameter aggregation from servers) completes entirely before sending averaged parameters to the next epoch — do not parallelize or reorder the averaging step with subsequent epoch processing

consequence.description: Skipping or parallelizing the averaging step causes stale or inconsistent parameters to propagate, corrupting federated learning convergence and producing models that do not represent true global consensus

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-074

core.when: When implementing warm-start functionality using coef_init or intercept_init parameters
core.action: Explicitly set warm_start=True before calling fit() to enable parameter reuse — without warm_start=True, coef_init and intercept_init only apply to the first fit() call and subsequent calls will reinitialize parameters, silently discarding warm-start behavior

consequence.description: If warm_start=False (default), coef_init and intercept_init parameters are ignored after the first fit() call, causing warm-start attempts to silently fail and lose previously learned parameter state

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-070

core.when: When consuming model output from lgdModel.fit()
core.action: Verify that the consumer code expects dictionary return type from fitted_params — if integrating with downstream systems, verify dict interface compatibility or implement explicit type handling; coordinate with team before changing return format to tuple or dataclass

consequence.description: Return format assumes dict interface consumer; if downstream systems expect a different type or the return format changes, data consumption breaks silently causing downstream processing failures

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-071

core.when: When implementing stateless server endpoints with warm-start functionality
core.action: Do not rely on stateless server architecture for /update endpoints that require warm-start — implement state persistence via coordinator tracking iteration state, sticky sessions with persistent server instances, or shared state store; parameters received via intercept_init/coef_init must be preserved across requests

consequence.description: Stateless server design initializes fresh state per request, but warm-start requires parameter state preservation; parameters passed via intercept_init/coef_init are silently discarded, causing the federated protocol to produce inconsistent models across rounds

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-082

core.when: When integrating data from multiple sources using X/Y column conventions
core.action: Implement schema validation for column mapping between openNPL API fields and X/Y conventions — verify that hardcoded field mappings (current_assets, cash_and_cash_equivalent_items) in dataSource function remain synchronized with upstream API schema; add explicit error handling if expected columns are missing or renamed

consequence.description: Hardcoded X/Y column convention breaks silently when openNPL API schema changes, causing incorrect feature extraction with no obvious error; downstream models train on misaligned data producing invalid LGD estimates

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-083

validation_threshold: openNPL API response missing 'current_assets' OR 'cash_and_cash_equivalent_items' → FAIL

core.when: When implementing federated learning coordination logic in the framework
core.action: Implement sequential blocking without timeout handling for inter-server ordering contracts — this creates a cascading deadlock vulnerability when servers have heterogeneous data volumes

consequence.description: Sequential blocking with no timeouts causes the federation to deadlock when any server experiences extended training time due to larger datasets; all participating servers hang waiting for the slowest server, causing complete federation failure

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-089

core.when: When implementing federated learning round coordination in the framework
core.action: Implement timeout handling for inter-server ordering contracts and add data volume heterogeneity checks before initiating coordination rounds

consequence.description: Without timeout handling and heterogeneity checks, the federation will experience cascading deadlocks when servers have significantly different dataset sizes, causing complete round failures and federation collapse

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-089

core.when: When implementing or modifying model serving logic
core.action: Initialize model from scratch for each request using server identifier — do not cache model state in server memory between requests

consequence.description: Stateful model servers cause backtest-live inconsistency: in containerized deployments, instances may be created/destroyed with stale cached state, and load-balanced multi-instance setups may route requests to instances with outdated models, leading to unpredictable execution results

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-038

validation_threshold: stateful_cache detected in model initialization → FAIL

core.when: When deploying model servers in containerized or load-balanced environments
core.action: Verify each request loads model parameters from persistent storage (server_dirs/{server_id}) independently — confirm no in-memory model caching across requests

consequence.description: In-memory caching of model state causes platform-dependent behavior: different container instances may have different cached states, making backtest results non-reproducible across deployment configurations

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-038

validation_threshold: class-level model variable with requestLifetime > 1 → FAIL

core.when: When implementing federated averaging weight configuration
core.action: Verify that sample_count is equal across each servers before using equal weights; if sample counts differ significantly, implement proportional weighting based on actual sample counts per server

consequence.description: Equal weighting (25% each) silently distorts federated model accuracy when servers have unequal data volumes; in production, servers with smaller datasets are over-weighted while larger datasets are under-weighted, leading to suboptimal model convergence and degraded prediction accuracy

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-013/BD-015

validation_threshold: sample_counts differ by more than 20% AND weights == equal_weights → WARN

core.when: When scaling the federated learning system beyond demo scale (>4 servers)
core.action: Replace blocking sequential HTTP requests with async parallel execution (asyncio with aiohttp) or thread pool to reduce latency from O(n) linear scaling to near-constant time

consequence.description: Sequential blocking communication creates linear latency growth O(n) with server count; for 8+ servers, round-trip time doubles compared to parallel execution, causing unacceptable delays in production federated training rounds

evidence_refs.summary: 742a770 Derived from BD: BD-016
